subject: [OOF] Move nginx to a sidecar container
commit_message:
[OOF] Move nginx to a sidecar container

Issue-ID: OPTFRA-794
Signed-off-by: krishnaa96 <krishna.moorthy6@wipro.com>
Change-Id: I16547f24ca58a80634ff13a9aeb7a5f595633b44

entries.message: Uploaded patch set 1.

Patch Set 1:

Gitlab controller: Deployment put on queue, number 1 on the queue.

Patch Set 1:

Gitlab Orange 1: Chained-CI build started, max time: 14400 seconds

Patch Set 1:

Build Started https://jenkins.onap.org/job/oom-master-verify-helm/5259/

Patch Set 1: Code-Review-1

Any reason to keep it as a sidecar instead of having it as a separated pod?
BTW
What's the role of this nginx? Couldn't it be replaced with ingress?

Patch Set 1:

> Patch Set 1: Code-Review-1
> 
> Any reason to keep it as a sidecar instead of having it as a separated pod?
> BTW
> What's the role of this nginx? Couldn't it be replaced with ingress?

The main application here is an uwsgi application which is simply an app server. It needs a web server to run. Nginx is playing the role of that web server.

Patch Set 1:

> Patch Set 1:
> 
> > Patch Set 1: Code-Review-1
> > 
> > Any reason to keep it as a sidecar instead of having it as a separated pod?
> > BTW
> > What's the role of this nginx? Couldn't it be replaced with ingress?
> 
> The main application here is an uwsgi application which is simply an app server. It needs a web server to run. Nginx is playing the role of that web server.

OK but then why not put nginx in a separate pod and let it speak to the service that just uses uwsgi protocol?

this way it is easier to debug as with sidecar container the pod is not failing as long as there is at least one sidecar running

Patch Set 1: Verified+1

Build Successful 

https://jenkins.onap.org/job/oom-master-verify-helm/5259/ : SUCCESS

Patch Set 1:

> Patch Set 1:
> 
> > Patch Set 1:
> > 
> > > Patch Set 1: Code-Review-1
> > > 
> > > Any reason to keep it as a sidecar instead of having it as a separated pod?
> > > BTW
> > > What's the role of this nginx? Couldn't it be replaced with ingress?
> > 
> > The main application here is an uwsgi application which is simply an app server. It needs a web server to run. Nginx is playing the role of that web server.
> 
> OK but then why not put nginx in a separate pod and let it speak to the service that just uses uwsgi protocol?
> 
> this way it is easier to debug as with sidecar container the pod is not failing as long as there is at least one sidecar running

Does this mean, the pod with uwsgi server is exposed via a kubernetes service and the nginx pod uses the service to communicate to the uswgi server?

Nginx also provides the SSL termination for the application. My concern is exposing uswgi app as a service directly. The uwsgi best practice suggests not to expose the socket speaking uswgi protocol to the external world. This channel allows for dynamic loading of applications which may lead to ACE attacks. 

I am not able to understand the second point. With multiple containers in a pod, if one of them fails, it will restart that container. Even with one container per pod, when the container fails, the pod will not fail. It will simply restart the container

Patch Set 1: -Code-Review

> Patch Set 1:
> 
> > Patch Set 1:
> > 
> > > Patch Set 1:
> > > 
> > > > Patch Set 1: Code-Review-1
> > > > 
> > > > Any reason to keep it as a sidecar instead of having it as a separated pod?
> > > > BTW
> > > > What's the role of this nginx? Couldn't it be replaced with ingress?
> > > 
> > > The main application here is an uwsgi application which is simply an app server. It needs a web server to run. Nginx is playing the role of that web server.
> > 
> > OK but then why not put nginx in a separate pod and let it speak to the service that just uses uwsgi protocol?
> > 
> > this way it is easier to debug as with sidecar container the pod is not failing as long as there is at least one sidecar running
> 
> Does this mean, the pod with uwsgi server is exposed via a kubernetes service and the nginx pod uses the service to communicate to the uswgi server?

Yes it is exposed but only as a ClusterIP not to the external world.

> 
> Nginx also provides the SSL termination for the application. My concern is exposing uswgi app as a service directly. The uwsgi best practice suggests not to expose the socket speaking uswgi protocol to the external world. This channel allows for dynamic loading of applications which may lead to ACE attacks. 

Sure that's clear for me. you shouldn't expose uswgi outside of the Cluster but you can always make this service cluster internal. If this is not enough you can always use kubernetes network policies and prevent any other services apart from you nginx from communicating with this service.

> 
> I am not able to understand the second point. With multiple containers in a pod, if one of them fails, it will restart that container. Even with one container per pod, when the container fails, the pod will not fail. It will simply restart the container

Right but I was rather afraid that the second container will be hanging and preventing the pod from entering the failed state but according to kubernetes doc it seems that failure of a single container is enough to make the whole pod fail.

Having said that I'm removing my -1 as this patch seems to be a step into good direction;)

Patch Set 3: Code-Review+2

Patch Set 3:

waiting for Krzysztof review
As Krzysztof, I think it would be better to have no sidecar nginx in the future (use ingress to finish ssl + clusterIP for python based code) but it's a step in the right direction

Patch Set 3: Code-Review+2

Fully agree with Sylvain. It's great that you entered the right path but please don't stop. Please continue;)
Change has been successfully merged by Krzysztof Opasiak
